ops.insert: Threat Intelligence for Ordinary People — a new category. It does not exist yet. We are founding it.

Every sector with frontline staff — NHS, retail, education, aviation, rail, legal, construction, finance, maritime, estate agency, NGOs — faces the same crisis. When a threat happens, the person in the moment has nothing. No guidance. No system. No real-time support.

Existing solutions fail because they solve the wrong problem: H&S/compliance platforms (SafetyCulture, Evotix) built for admin desks. Lone worker tools (StaySafe, Peoplesafe) track location but give no guidance. eLearning (iHasco, Skillcast) one-hour video, zero behaviour change.

None of them are built by people who have operated under real threat. None of them combine training, real-time response, and intelligence in one system. None of them meet the person in the moment. We do. We are the only ones who can.

The three-layer model: (1) Training — military-grade behavioural training, progressive, builds lasting change (2) Platform — Absolute Defence OS, always-on AI-powered threat response system (3) Intelligence — aggregated anonymised threat patterns, sector benchmarking, predictive risk
The flywheel: Training creates awareness → Platform reinforces behaviour in live situations → Data reveals patterns organisations can't see → Intelligence improves training → repeat
The moat: Thirty years of lived operational experience. No software company can manufacture that. No PE-backed platform can acquire it.
UK Workplace Safety Market: USD $1.2B (2024), forecast USD $2.7B by 2030 at 14.5% CAGR — Grand View Research. Includes PPE, software, compliance training, incident management.
UK Occupational Health & Safety Services: £1.9B (2026) — IBISWorld. Consultancy, audit, training, compliance services.
Corporate Training Market (Global): USD $360B and growing — Category Creation Brief cites £3.2B UK training market + £1.4B H&S software market.
European Lone Worker Safety Solutions: €285M (2024), forecast €180M Europe / €100M North America by 2029. UK accounts for almost HALF of total European market. 1.2M users growing to 1.8M+ by 2029.
Smart Personal Safety & Security Devices (Global): forecast USD $74.9B by 2032 — SNS Insider. AI-based threat detection driving growth.
North America Mass Notification Systems: USD $8.1B (2025), forecast $19.8B by 2030 at 19.5% CAGR — MarketsandMarkets. Everbridge plays here.

OUR POSITION: We sit at the intersection of all three markets — training + platform + intelligence. No existing vendor combines them. That's why this is a new category, not a feature in an existing one.
642,000 workplace violence incidents in the UK in 2023/24 — HSE
41,226 physical assaults on NHS staff in 2023 — up 21% since 2019
78% of retail workers verbally assaulted in 2025 (BRC/USDAW). 43,000 physically violent incidents in retail Sept 2024-Aug 2025 — BRC Crime Survey 2026
35% increase in aggressive passenger incidents in aviation — IATA 2023
40% rise in public confrontations on rail networks — RSSB 2023
86% of tradespeople report customer abuse; 10% face physical attacks
32% increase in violent incidents in UK schools — over 5 years
Nearly 50% of women in the UK feel unsafe walking alone at night
Employees with regular refresher training respond 40% faster in emergencies
Culture Shift 2026 survey: UK employees are scared to speak up about bad behaviour — fear of retaliation, lack of anonymous channels. Our Voice Journal directly addresses this.
Martyn's Law (Terrorism Protection of Premises) — mandates threat assessments for all venues 100+ capacity. Fines up to £18M. Creates MANDATORY market for exactly what we sell.

Duty of care litigation rising — employers increasingly held liable for foreseeable workplace violence. Corporate manslaughter charges where risk data existed but wasn't acted on. Our heatmap + acknowledgement chain = documented due diligence.
AI-powered personal safety — market moving from reactive location tracking to predictive, AI-driven threat intelligence. Lone worker tools being disrupted by smarter platforms. We lead this shift.

Training failure crisis — BRC data shows violence against shopworkers fell 20% where proper training was deployed. But 90%+ of organisations still rely on annual eLearning tick-box exercises that produce zero behaviour change. Our progressive in-person training model fills this gap.

Anonymous reporting demand surging — Culture Shift research (2026) shows employees fear speaking up. Organisations deploying anonymous channels see 3-5x more concern submissions than staff-entered systems. Our QR + Voice Journal removes every barrier.

Insurance-led safety tech adoption — insurers offering premium reductions for organisations with documented proactive risk management. Our platform produces exactly the evidence insurers want: heatmaps, timed response logs, compliance audit trails.

Platform convergence — organisations tired of siloed tools (one for reporting, one for training, one for compliance, one for lone workers). Want single platform that covers training + response + intelligence. We are the only vendor offering all three layers.
HQ: Burlington, MA, USA. NASDAQ-listed (until taken private by Thoma Bravo 2023). Recognised as Leader in Forrester Wave™ CEM. Claims 358% ROI.

What they do: Mass notification + critical event management platform. Everbridge 360™ — three tiers (Core/Professional/Enterprise). Covers mass alerts, risk intelligence, crisis management, Visual Command Center.
Target customers: Fortune 500, government agencies, global enterprises (Sanofi, Goldman Sachs, HSBC, Siemens). Public safety agencies for IPAWS/national warning systems.
Pricing: Enterprise-only. Six-figure annual contracts (£100k-£500k+). Per-user licensing. Setup fees, customisation costs, training packages all extra. PAI Consulting calls it 'annual vendor ransom'.
Strengths: Proven at massive scale. Carrier-level SMS/voice redundancy. Global threat intelligence feeds. IPAWS integration. ISO 27001, SOC 2 certified. Brand credibility with boards and auditors.

Weaknesses: Designed for corporate security teams, NOT frontline staff. No in-the-moment guidance. No de-escalation training. No anonymous reporting. Clunky UX — described as 'workflows buried under layers of clicks'. 6-12 month implementations. Requires dedicated admin team. Collects personal mobile numbers (privacy headache). Zero behavioural training component.

What they DON'T do: No situational response guidance. No real-time threat heatmap at building level. No QR-triggered instant reporting. No SOS emergency protocol. No AI triage of individual incidents. No voice journal / wellbeing channel. No military-grade behavioural training. No sector-specific adaptation.

Attack angle: Everbridge tells the control room what happened. Absolute Defence tells the PERSON IN THE MOMENT what to do. They broadcast alerts. We provide intelligence. They cost £100k+/yr. We deploy in 48 hours for a fraction. They need a security team to operate. We need a QR code on the wall.
HQ: New York City. VC-backed (raised $133M+). Consumer app with 10M+ users. Recently expanded to 15 new US cities (Mar 2026). Enterprise tier launched: Citizen Enterprise.

What they do: Real-time location-based incident alerts from police/fire radio dispatch and CAD feeds. Claims alerts up to 45 minutes before traditional systems. Live user-streamed incident videos. 20+ incident categories. Enterprise version adds asset tracking for alert zones and team notification.
Target customers: Enterprise — real estate, retail, education, medical, security teams. Consumer — individuals in US metro areas. Primarily US-focused.
Pricing: Free 14-day enterprise trial. Enterprise pricing undisclosed (demo required). Consumer app is free with premium subscription options.

Strengths: Massive user-generated content network (10M+ users streaming live video). Real-time police/fire radio integration. Extremely fast awareness of public safety events. Clean modern UI. Strong US metro coverage. Team sharing and notification features.

Weaknesses: AWARENESS ONLY — tells you WHAT is happening nearby but gives ZERO guidance on what to DO about it. No de-escalation scripts. No response guidance. No training component whatsoever. No anonymous internal reporting system. No incident documentation/audit trail for compliance. No building-level zone intelligence. US-centric — no UK police/fire radio feeds, no UK regulatory compliance. No AI triage of internal concerns. No wellbeing channel. Crowdsourced content creates noise and potential misinformation risk.

What they DON'T do: No in-the-moment response guidance. No SOS emergency protocol with silent logging. No situational guide with de-escalation and legal advice. No compliance engine (HSE, Ofsted, CQC, ISO 45001). No incident vault with audit trail. No heatmap at organisational level. No anonymous internal voice/text reporting. No military-grade behavioural training. No sector-specific customisation. No UK presence.

Attack angle: Citizen is a SCANNER — it tells you there's a fire nearby. Absolute Defence is a SHIELD — it tells your staff exactly what to do when the fire is IN YOUR BUILDING. They monitor the street. We protect the person. They aggregate public incidents. We manage YOUR incidents. They have no training, no guidance, no compliance, no audit trail. We have all four.

HQ: New York City. NASDAQ-listed (OBAI). Market cap ~$27M (Mar 2026). Debuted on NASDAQ Feb 2026. $100M+ invested to date. 28 countries. 1.4M+ security service requests handled including 10,000+ emergencies. App Store rating 4.8/5.

What they do: AI-powered 'Preventative Personal Security' — claims to be creating a new category. Combines AI with 24/7 human Personal Security Agents in command centres. Features: Ready An Agent (panic hold), Video Monitor Me (live video watched by security agent), Track Me On The Go (journey monitoring), Security Check (scheduled check-ins), Send Me A Car, Siren, Bodyguard booking. Land-and-expand enterprise model.

Target customers: Enterprise employees (lone workers, commuters, WFH), universities, cities, families. Recent wins: Top-3 US telecom, $300B+ pharma leader, trillion-dollar multinational, European fintech firms. Positioning for both corporate duty-of-care and individual consumer security.
Pricing: Turnkey service — 'no setup time or costs'. Enterprise per-employee model. $250K ARR from single pharma deal. Potential $10M+ ARR from largest pilot. Consumer subscription available.

Strengths: Closest conceptual overlap with Absolute Defence — both claiming 'new category'. Heavyweight advisory board (former US Secret Service director, former NYPD commissioner, former FBI deputy director, former Israeli VIP protection head). 24/7 live human security agents. Strong testimonials from women/lone workers. Globally scalable (28 countries, multiple languages). Real life-saving track record. NASDAQ visibility gives investor credibility.

Weaknesses: PERSONAL protection model — designed for individuals OUTSIDE buildings. Zero organisational intelligence. No building-level zone mapping or heatmap. No anonymous internal reporting system. No incident vault or compliance audit trail. No in-situ response guidance (de-escalation scripts, legal position, immediate action steps). No training component AT ALL. No wellbeing/voice journal channel. Requires dedicated mobile app download (barrier to adoption). Very expensive human-agent model — doesn't scale cost-effectively for frontline staff like retail/NHS/education. $27M market cap despite $100M invested — market is questioning viability. Stock has fallen 94.91% from 52-week high. No UK regulatory compliance (Martyn's Law, KCSIE, CQC, HSE).

What they DON'T do: No situational guide with threat-specific response steps. No QR-triggered reporting (no app required). No building/site-level threat intelligence. No AI triage of internal concerns. No compliance engine for regulators. No knowledge vault for policies. No SOS that also creates a silent incident log for the organisation. No sector-specific customisation. No behavioural training. No data layer for aggregated threat pattern intelligence.

Attack angle: Bond protects INDIVIDUALS — one person, one app, one bodyguard-on-call. Absolute Defence protects ORGANISATIONS — every building, every zone, every person in that space, all at once. They need a £10/mo app download per employee. We need a QR poster on the wall. They have ex-Secret Service advisors. We have operators who've actually been in hostile environments. They burned $100M building a personal bodyguard app. We built a complete organisational threat intelligence system on Taskade Genesis for a fraction. They protect you walking home. We protect you AT WORK, IN THE BUILDING, IN THE MOMENT — and we prove it to your regulator.
Competitive Matrix — Absolute Defence vs. The Field
Military-grade behavioural training: Everbridge ✗ | Citizen ✗ | Bond ✗ | Absolute Defence ✅ — No competitor has any training component built by actual operators

Real-time in-the-moment threat guidance: Everbridge ✗ | Citizen ✗ | Bond ✗ | Absolute Defence ✅ — Everbridge sends mass alerts. Citizen shows nearby events. Bond calls you a human agent. None give structured de-escalation scripts, legal position, and step-by-step actions.
AI incident triage (ARIA): Everbridge ✗ | Citizen ✗ | Bond ✗ | Absolute Defence ✅ — No competitor analyses every submission, flags risk, and cross-references a knowledge base in real time

Emergency panic routing (SOS + silent log): Everbridge Partial | Citizen ✗ | Bond Partial | Absolute Defence ✅ — Bond has a panic button but no organisational incident log. Everbridge has mass notification but no one-tap personal SOS with GPS-stamped silent record.
Anonymous wellbeing channel (Voice Journal): Everbridge ✗ | Citizen ✗ | Bond ✗ | Absolute Defence ✅ — No competitor offers anonymous voice/text submissions analysed by AI for pattern detection
QR-triggered no-app reporting: Everbridge ✗ | Citizen ✗ | Bond ✗ | Absolute Defence ✅ — Every competitor requires an app download or login. We require a 30-second QR scan.

Audit-ready compliance data (HSE/Ofsted/CQC/ISO 45001): Everbridge Partial | Citizen ✗ | Bond ✗ | Absolute Defence ✅ — Everbridge has analytics but not UK safeguarding/duty-of-care compliance. Citizen and Bond have zero compliance engine.

Live threat heatmap by zone/location: Everbridge Partial (Visual Command Center) | Citizen ✗ (shows nearby events, no org-level) | Bond ✗ | Absolute Defence ✅ — Only AD provides recency-weighted, building-level zone risk with temporal pattern detection

Built by operators with real hostile environment experience: Everbridge ✗ (software company) | Citizen ✗ (tech startup) | Bond ✗ (tech company with advisory board) | Absolute Defence ✅ — Founded by commissioned officer with 4 operational tours and 30 years in self-protection systems
Deployment speed: Everbridge 6-12 months | Citizen days (alerts only) | Bond days (app only) | Absolute Defence 48 hours — Full platform with zones, QR codes, AI, heatmap, compliance
Per-site entry cost: Everbridge £100k+/yr | Citizen undisclosed | Bond £10+/mo per user (scales fast) | Absolute Defence sub-£5k/yr per site — 10-100x cheaper than enterprise alternatives

THE FUNDAMENTAL GAP: Everbridge protects the ORGANISATION from macro events. Citizen reports what's happening on the STREET. Bond protects the INDIVIDUAL when they're alone. NONE of them protect the PERSON IN THE MOMENT, IN THE BUILDING, WITH GUIDANCE A TRAINED PROFESSIONAL WOULD GIVE THEM. Only Absolute Defence does that. That is the category we are creating.

Segment H: Finance, Legal & Estate Agency

Martyn's Law (Terrorism Protection of Premises Bill)
Status: Royal Assent expected 2025. Enforcement phased — Standard Tier (100-799 capacity) first, Enhanced Tier (800+) with heavier duties
Standard Tier requirements: terrorism risk assessment, public protection procedures, staff training records, incident reporting mechanism
Enhanced Tier (800+): all above plus security coordinator, detailed vulnerability assessment, monitoring duties, regulator reporting
Penalties: fines up to £18M or 5% of revenue for non-compliance. Personal liability for responsible persons.
Absolute Defence advantage: our zone mapping, threat heatmap, incident reporting, and staff guide mode map directly onto every Martyn's Law requirement
KCSIE — Keeping Children Safe in Education
Updated annually by DfE — all schools must comply. Ofsted inspects safeguarding compliance as pass/fail gate
Key requirements: chronological recording, information sharing, staff training log, concern categorisation, multi-agency referral tracking
Absolute Defence advantage: automated chronological audit trail, concern type taxonomy, AI-suggested categorisation, exportable Ofsted-ready reports
Safeguarding data is special category data — requires explicit lawful basis (vital interests / public task / legitimate interests)
Data minimisation principle applies — collect only what's necessary for safeguarding purpose
Right to erasure vs safeguarding retention — safeguarding records exempt from deletion requests when child protection is at stake
Absolute Defence advantage: role-based access control, encrypted storage, audit log of who viewed what, data retention policies built in
Health & Safety at Work Act + Duty of Care
Employers have legal duty to ensure health, safety and welfare of employees — includes protection from violence and threats
Corporate manslaughter liability if foreseeable risks not addressed — incident heatmap data becomes evidence of due diligence
Absolute Defence advantage: demonstrable risk assessment (heatmap), documented response procedures (guide mode), timed acknowledgement chain = legal protection
Starter Tier (£199/mo): single site, up to 5 zones, incident vault, QR reporting, basic analytics — ideal for individual schools/small venues
Professional Tier (£499/mo): up to 20 zones, AI threat intelligence (ARIA), heatmap, guide mode, voice journals, acknowledgement chain
Enterprise Tier (custom): multi-site, unlimited zones, API integrations, dedicated support, custom compliance modules, SLA
MAT/Group discount: 15% discount for 10+ sites, 25% for 30+ sites — incentivises trust-wide adoption
Pricing psychology: position against cost of a single serious incident (£50k-£500k legal/reputational) — system pays for itself with one prevented escalation
"We can't afford another system" → What's the cost of a safeguarding failure? Serious Case Review = £200k+. Our annual cost is less than one supply teacher day per month.
"Our staff won't use another tool" → QR scan takes 30 seconds. No login required. No training needed. Show them the public report form — it's simpler than sending a text.
"We need to go through procurement" → We're G-Cloud listed (target). Here's our DPIA. Here's our penetration test certificate. We've done the compliance homework for you.
"What about data security?" → UK-hosted, encrypted at rest and in transit, role-based access, full audit trail, GDPR compliant by design. Your data never leaves UK jurisdiction.
"Martyn's Law might not apply to us" → If you have 100+ capacity at any event, it applies. And even if it doesn't — duty of care does. This protects you either way.

"We've looked at Citizen" → Citizen shows you what's happening on the street outside. We protect the people INSIDE your building. They have no training, no guidance, no compliance, no audit trail. And they don't operate in the UK.

"What about Bond?" → Bond is a personal bodyguard app. They protect individual executives walking home. We protect EVERY person in your building — visitors, staff, contractors — with a QR code on the wall. No app download needed. And we train your people to handle threats, not just call for help.

"We have lone worker devices (StaySafe/Peoplesafe)" → They track your location. They don't tell you what to do when someone threatens you. They don't train your staff. They don't create organisational intelligence. They're a GPS dot. We're a defence system.

"We do annual eLearning (iHasco/Skillcast)" → Research shows eLearning produces zero lasting behaviour change. Our training is delivered by operators who've been in real hostile environments. Progressive, not one-off. And the platform reinforces that training every single day on the job.
Min 0-1: Open with the question — "When was the last incident at your site? How long did it take to document?"
Min 1-2: Show the live threat heatmap — "This is what your risk landscape looks like right now. Every dot is a real incident."
Min 2-3: Scan a QR code live — "Your visitor just reported a concern. Watch it appear in real-time. No app download. No login."
Min 3-4: Trigger the SOS — "Someone is in danger. One tap. Emergency services called. Your safety lead notified. Silent log created. All in 3 seconds."
Min 4-5: Open ARIA — "Ask ARIA what to do about an aggressive intruder. Watch the AI guide you through de-escalation and legal response in real-time."
Min 5-6: Show the acknowledgement chain — "This incident was logged 4 minutes ago. The system is counting down. If no one acknowledges in 15 minutes, it auto-escalates."
Min 6-7: Close — "What would it mean for your organisation to have all of this running by next week? We deploy in 48 hours."

7. Competitive Differentiators — Why Absolute Defence Wins
ARIA AI Engine — only platform with built-in safeguarding AI that analyses incidents, flags patterns, and provides real-time guidance. Not bolted-on ChatGPT — purpose-trained for threat intelligence.
Live Threat Heatmap — spatial, temporal, recency-weighted risk visualisation. No competitor can show you where danger concentrates and when. This is the "wow" moment in every demo.
QR-Triggered Anonymous Reporting — anyone can report. No app. No login. No barrier. Scan, type, submit. 30 seconds. This unlocks intelligence that staff-only systems never capture.
SOS Emergency Protocol — one-tap emergency activation with automatic 999 dial, silent incident logging, and instant safety lead notification. No competitor has this.

Situational Guide Mode — staff scan a QR code and get immediate, legally-informed response guidance for 8 threat scenarios. De-escalation scripts, legal boundaries, step-by-step actions. Like having a crisis consultant in your pocket.

Acknowledgement Chain — timed escalation engine. If an incident isn't acknowledged in 15 minutes, it escalates. If tier 2 doesn't respond in 30 minutes, compliance breach logged. Accountability is automatic.
Cross-Sector Architecture — built for schools, councils, venues, hospitals, corporate from day one. Competitors are locked into education. We scale across every sector that Martyn's Law touches.
48-Hour Deployment — no IT team required. No server setup. No training programme. Upload your site map, print QR codes, go live. Competitors take weeks or months.
One sentence: The first intelligent system that combines military-grade training with always-on AI-powered threat response — built by operators who've been in the field.
The gap in one question: When something serious happens in your building tomorrow — does your team know exactly what to do, in real time, without having to think?

For investors: New category creation. £3.2B training market + £1.4B H&S software + growing lone worker sector. Recurring SaaS + per-site licensing + premium intelligence tier. Sticky — once incident data lives in the platform, clients cannot leave.
For HR / People Teams: Staff confidence, retention, reduced absenteeism. Measurable safety culture improvement.
For Operations / Risk Managers: Live incident command. Complete audit trail. Regulator-ready documentation.
For CEOs / Leadership: Duty of care delivered as data. Insurance, legal, and reputational protection.
For Safety Leads: ARIA handles triage. You handle decisions. Your workload halves.
For Insurers: Documented proactive risk management changes the premium conversation.
For Regulators: Ofsted, CQC, HSE, ISO 45001 — every audit trail in one place.
